item.title: I bet this guy gets good tips
item.dc:creator: DropDrop

item.title: I don't have anything nice to say, actually
item.description: I think sometimes we try to paint people into a box; good people are all good and bad people are all bad. But people who are good and loving and kind to their families sometimes treat people outside of their families like shit. Sometimes, good people - who are otherwise kind and generous - support policies that leave people unhoused, enslaved, disenfranchised, and dying. I don't believe in Jesus, but I used to, and it especially annoys me when these complicated people do. If someone claims Jesus in their beliefs, but I can't see him in their actions, I secretly hope they get to hear it from the horses mouth when they die. I hope they meet the Jesus who told them their wealth would bar them from heaven. I hope they have to answer to a god who told them to sacrifice everything to protect their neighbors and foreigners, as well as their loved ones. I know it is a toxic impulse to wish that fate on others. I just don't know how else to express my disappointment. It feels like saying, "l wish Santa Claus WAS real, so he could leave you coal." Is just a weird impulse I have. In general, I don't believe in an afterlife; if there is one, I hope it is as precious as this life, or that it is a comfort after the difficulties of this life. Or both, really.
item.dc:creator: youngfarty

item.title: Steve Miller to Eminem: “I’m Honored.”
item.description:
* Video Below * Steve Miller posted this message to Eminem regarding his recent sample/cover of Miller’s 1982 hit song, “Abracadabra.”
Steve Miller Band Sauce: https://youtu.be/Md0m_tiJnks?si=eFHAodf7voYNDIFb
Eminem Sauce: https://youtu.be/22tVWwmTie8?si=684fnO3cIxPF9QxP

item.dc:creator: IMSALTYRICK

item.title: My wife IRL
item.description: True story, when my wife was pregnant she ordered over 100 dollars worth of Chinese food (this was in 2010). I thought she ordered individual entrees, but it wasn't! They were all DINNER ORDERS. When they delivered, the person delivering asked if we were having a party or something and I said, "No, why?" Guy just laughed and said here ya go. I thought we would have enough for a few days, but I underestimated the ability of a pregnant woman. We had no leftovers, she devoured it all.
item.dc:creator: zMike70z

item.title: Love heals
item.description: Abused and abandoned, every gentle touch assured him that, even in his darkest moments, he was going to be OK. Love heals, it truly does.

item.title: Monday Summer Meme dump to help you trough the heat wave (no politics)
item.description:
1
2. Be a smooth as this dude
3. Robber vs Store lady
4
5. In case you need some morning sweat
6. Who is she?!
7. What's wrong with your cat?
8. Do you like how young people dressed back in the day?
9. American car vs. Straight Road
10
11. Women: "Men are liars" Also women:
12
13. Paranormal cativity caught on camera
14. That's the kind of pranks I'm all in for
15. If you have nothing better to do on a Monday day you can try the demo of my "Stanley Parable"-inspired game. I think the humor and the cold distant voice of the narrator will keep you good company ! Plus you get not to press a button! Can you imagine all the fun you can have not doing that? It's one for the ages! You can try on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. It's a real game, I swear! (Pls wishlist uwu)
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: USA had Mr Rogers, Germany had Peter Lustig
item.description: My favorite children TV show named Löwenzahn / "Dandelion". Peter Lustig ( his real name) was the host and he explained us kids everything. Every episode had a theme and he talked about that and explained it. At the end of every episode he would say something like, " and now, turn off the TV, its enough, go out and play, do something else". An important part of my childhood during the 80s. It's still running today with a different host.
item.dc:creator: MaterialGirl1

item.title: Mood swings and headaches gallore...
item.description: A quick calculating says that I've regularly been ingesting twice the daily recommended maximum caffeine. I've spent the whole year trying to ease out slowly to try to avoid the heaviest withdrawal symptoms. It worked, but the once that I got were still a bitch. Depression, mood swings, headaches and migraines. Last week I only drank about what would previously have been just a days worth of soda, this week I didn't drink a single one.
item.dc:creator: WeaselBandit

item.title: The Universe giveth, the Universe taketh
item.description: Went to a motorcyle event yesterday. Specifically for the raffle. Both me and BF have never won shit in our lives and we were joking how ironic it would be, since last year we got a 2nd bike so we wouldn't have to share just my old one. After a very awkward acceptance, I think I was drunk with joy and surprise. When we left the event, I miscalculated a gravel patch. I wasn't even driving fast yet. Bike slid, my ankle underneath. I've never had a broken bone, but I was able to still walk on said foot, albeit in pain. Decided to visit the emergency room to check. To everyone's surprise, doc included, broken fibula. 1 month on my arse. Bike won is the new KTM 790 Adventure. So many mixed emotions, especially since I've had some extremely shit few years. Edit to clarify: the bike I slipped with is my old one, of 8 years. And it's fine
item.dc:creator: SaintLuciferVonAllah

item.dc:creator: fponias

item.title: My sad little guy yowling for me while I was out having breakfast.
item.description: Kevin loves his jacuzzi full of packing paper, and I thought it would be fun to record some of his antics, but I didn’t expect to catch him crying for me while I was gone. Now that this has hit “Most Viral”, I feel like I need to tell everyone that Kevin is one of 3 cats, so I am well aware of the benefits of having more than one cat.
item.dc:creator: RadioactiveNerfDart

item.title: I think I can say former feral for this one!
item.description: Yesterday the little boy went to his new home. I'm so happy for him and his people! But was also a little sad for me. Today I was rewarded with the first feral snuggle! My butt and feet were numb, my back sore. But my heart was melting!
